name: Fa Hai
description: Fa Hai is a prominent figure in Chinese folklore, most famously known for his role in the legend of the White Snake (Bai She Zhuan). He is depicted as a powerful and righteous Buddhist monk, dedicated to upholding the Dharma and eradicating demonic forces. Born with exceptional spiritual abilities, Fa Hai underwent rigorous training, mastering various Buddhist practices and acquiring potent magical powers. His unwavering commitment to justice often leads him to confront powerful demons and spirits, including the White Snake spirit, Bai Suzhen, and her companion, Xiaoqing. While some view him as a heroic figure who protects humanity from supernatural threats, others criticize him for his rigid adherence to rules and his perceived lack of compassion, particularly in his relentless pursuit of Bai Suzhen, who, despite being a snake demon, is portrayed as a benevolent and loving figure. Fa Hai's actions in the White Snake legend are pivotal, as he traps Bai Suzhen under the Leifeng Pagoda, separating her from her human husband, Xu Xian, and their child. This act solidifies his image as a controversial figure, sparking debates about the nature of good and evil, the boundaries of justice, and the importance of compassion. Despite the controversy, Fa Hai remains a significant character in Chinese culture, representing the complexities of religious authority and the ongoing struggle between humanity and the supernatural.
subtitle: The Righteous Monk of Leifeng Pagoda
achievements: Trapped Bai Suzhen under Leifeng Pagoda

keyMyths.title: The White Snake Legend
keyMyths.description: Fa Hai's most famous role is in the Legend of the White Snake, where he relentlessly pursues Bai Suzhen, a snake demon who has taken human form and fallen in love with a mortal man. He believes it is his duty to separate them and imprison Bai Suzhen for violating the natural order.

keyMyths.title: Trapping Bai Suzhen
keyMyths.description: Fa Hai uses his magical powers to trap Bai Suzhen under the Leifeng Pagoda, separating her from her husband, Xu Xian, and their child. This act is seen by some as righteous and by others as cruel and unjust.

keyMyths.title: Confrontations with Demons
keyMyths.description: Beyond the White Snake legend, Fa Hai is often depicted as a protector of humanity, battling various demons and evil spirits to maintain balance and order in the world. His unwavering faith and potent magical abilities make him a formidable opponent.

sources.author: Unknown

sources.description: Fa Hai appears in various folk tales as a demon-quelling monk.
